channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Escalating Conflict in the Middle East: Strait of Hormuz Blockade and Regional Tensions
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have intensified as the war involving the United States, Israel, and Iran enters its third week. Israel has launched targeted ground operations in southern Lebanon, prompti...

item.title: Exploring Tasmania’s West Coast: History, Wilderness, and Scenic Wonders
item.description: Tasmania’s West Coast offers a blend of rugged wilderness, tragic history, and unique attractions. The Woolnorth property, once part of the Van Diemen’s Land Company, features heritage outbuildings, 4...

item.title: Trump Claims Iran Uses AI to Mislead Public Amid War Tensions
item.description: On Sunday, former US President Donald Trump claimed that Iran is using artificial intelligence as a tool to spread disinformation about its war activities and achievements. Speaking to journalists on ...

item.title: Understanding Fathers’ Roles in Supporting Children with Intellectual Disabilities in Kenya
item.description: This article talks about how fathers take care of children with disabilities in Kenya, focusing especially on kids with intellectual challenges like Down syndrome, epilepsy, or autism. Research shows ...

item.title: Pakistan Stock Market Plummets as Oil Prices Surge Amid Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: Pakistan’s stock market experienced a significant downturn on Monday, with the KSE-100 index shedding a substantial 4,687.50 points – a drop of over 3%. This marked a turbulent start to the trading we...

item.title: Filipino Students Targeted for Extremist Indoctrination Through Roblox
item.description: A recent report and video segment by GMA’s Resibo highlighted how the Philippine National Police's Anti-Cybercrime Group (ACG) intervened to prevent a planned school attack in Calabarzon, Philippines....

item.title: Strengthening Early Literacy Across South Africa
item.description: South Africa faces a critical moment in its educational journey as highlighted by the 2030 Reading Panel’s 2026 report. The data from the Funda Uphumelele National Survey (FUNS) reveals that only arou...

item.title: Israel Conducts Strikes in South Lebanon Amid Escalating Iran Conflict; Casualties Reported
item.description: The conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States continues to escalate, affecting multiple regions in the Middle East. Early reports from Lebanon indicate that Israeli airstrikes in the sout...

item.title: Edong Supports Lucas’ Strategy Against Crisanto in Latest Episode
item.description: In the latest episode of 'What Lies Beneath,' tensions rise as Edong decides to support Lucas' strategy against Crisanto. The episode highlights the complex dynamics between the characters, showing ho...

item.title: Three arrested following discovery of a man's body in a park bin in Coventry
item.description: Three individuals have been arrested in connection with the discovery of a man's body in a council wheelie bin in Coventry. The body was found in Cash's Park, off Daimler Road, by a member of the publ...

item.title: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory: Big Data and the Future of Astronomy
item.description: Modern astronomy is increasingly dependent on enormous datasets and automated analysis, exemplified by the Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile. Its Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) will map the s...

item.title: AIRC Empowers 600 Schoolgirls in Rivers with Sanitary Pads and Menstrual Hygiene Education
item.description: African Immigrants Resources Centre (AIRC), wey be NGO wey dey USA, don give over 600 schoolgirls for Rivers State sanitary pads and correct education on how to take care of dem menstrual hygiene. Dis...

item.title: Challenges of Remote Work for Indian IT Professionals in Global Companies
item.description: Indian IT professionals, numbering around five million, are crucial to maintaining the digital operations of major Western firms. Despite often working for globally renowned companies, many face diffi...

item.title: Democratic candidates face scrutiny over past 'Winter Texan' remarks
item.description: The Fox News article reports that several Democratic candidates have faced renewed attention over past comments referring to 'Winter Texans,' a term used to describe seasonal visitors from northern st...

item.title: Seven women die as roof collapses at BISP payment shop in Rahim Yar Khan
item.description: In a tragic incident in Rahim Yar Khan’s Chak 123-P Tibba area, at least seven women lost their lives and many others sustained injuries when the roof of a shop collapsed on Monday morning. The shop w...

item.title: Court Reschedules Raphael Tuju’s Case Against East African Development Bank
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju’s legal battle with the East African Development Bank (EADB) hit an unexpected snag on Monday, March 16, 2026. Tuju’s team was prepared for a 9 a.m virtual hearin...

item.title: Three Fishermen from NSW Die in Boating Accident off South Australia’s Limestone Coast
item.description: Three men from Coleambally in New South Wales tragically lost their lives during a fishing trip on South Australia’s Limestone Coast. Les DeMamiel, 74, Russell Fisher, 67, and John McKenzie, 65, went ...

item.title: Indomie launches AI-powered platform to create personalised praise songs for mothers
item.description: For dis year Mother’s Day, Indomie don use technology blend with culture for something wey no too dey common. Dem don launch platform wey go allow people upload picture of their mama, put her name, ch...

item.title: Provincial bus fare hike forces low-income commuters to make tough choices amid rising fuel costs
item.description: A recent increase in provincial bus fares, which took effect on March 14, 2026, has placed a heavy burden on low-income Filipinos, especially those traveling between Metro Manila and their home provin...

item.title: Iran Allows Some Countries’ Ships Safe Passage Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Conflict
item.description: Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz open for shipping to all countries except the United States and its allies amid the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Tehran. The strait, a crucial route for abou...
